title: The two-cell P0 projection is L2-stable

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The squared L2 energy of the two-cell P0 projection is no larger than the original squared L2 energy.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real cell values
the P0 projection is the two-cell average on both cells
the squared L2 energy is the sum of the two squared coordinates
informal.expected_conclusion: The projected squared energy is bounded above by the original squared energy.

declarations.name: cellL2Sq2
declarations.statement: def cellL2Sq2 (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 ^ 2 + u1 ^ 2

declarations.name: p0Projection2_l2_contract
declarations.statement: theorem p0Projection2_l2_contract (u0 u1 : Real) : cellL2Sq2 (p0Projection2Left u0 u1) (p0Projection2Right u0 u1) <= cellL2Sq2 u0 u1

tasks.input: Translate L2 stability of the two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem p0Projection2_l2_contract (u0 u1 : Real) : cellL2Sq2 (p0Projection2Left u0 u1) (p0Projection2Right u0 u1) <= cellL2Sq2 u0 u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by rewriting with the Pythagorean identity and using nonnegativity of squared residuals.
tasks.target: Rewrite by p0Projection2_pythagorean and use sq_nonneg with nlinarith for the residual energy.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics identity that decomposes original energy into projected energy plus residual energy.
